original: Edmund Hillary(NZ) and Tenzing Norgay (Nepal) are the first to reach the summit of Mount Everest as part of a British expedition
World's smallest surviving baby, a girl, discharged from Sharp March Birch Hospital in San Diego after being born at 23 weeks weighing 8.6 ounces (245 grams)
Space Shuttle Discovery completes the first docking with the International Space Station
Man disguised as an elderly disabled woman throws cake at the Mona Lisa at the Louvre in Paris, painting undamaged
Yuri Dumchev of USSR throws discus a record 71.86 m

Albert Einstein's Theory of Relativity, that when light passes a large body, gravity will bend the rays confirmed by Arthur Eddington's expedition to photograph a solar eclipse on the island of Principe, West Africa
Great iceball comet seen above North Sea
Spanish scientists announce a new human species in a 780,000-year-old fossil
The Korean navy, led by Admiral Yi Sun-sin, repels a Japanese fleet in the Battle of Sacheon, the first use of a Korean Turtle Ship
American pharmacistJohn Pembertonbegins advertising his patented medicine, Coca-Cola, in Atlanta, Georgia
"Jurassic World", directed by Colin Trevorrow and starringChris Prattand Bryce Dallas Howard premieres in Paris
Constantinople, capital of the Eastern Roman Empire, falls to the Ottoman Turks underMehmed the Conqueror, ending the Byzantine Empire after 1,100 years
American long jumperRalph Bostonsets then world record 27' 4 3/4" (8.35m) at Modesto, California
Australian Paul McManus water skis barefoot for 1:30:19
Amputee Steve Fonyo completes cross-Canada marathon at Victoria, British Columbia, after 14 months
First North Pole flight in a single-engine plane completed by Charles F. Blair Jr., who flies 3,260 nonstop miles across the North Pole from Bardufoss, Norway, to Fairbanks, Alaska
MLB Oakland A's outfielderRickey Hendersonsteals record 893rd base, breakingTy Cobb's record
Larry Birdbeats outMagic Johnsonfor NBA rookie of year
Lincoln says "You can fool some of the people all of the time, and all of the people some of the time, but you can not fool all of the people all of the time."
Saunders-Roe SR.N1, the first practical hovercraft, performs its first engine run
Britain's Trans-Arctic expedition makes 1st crossing of Arctic Sea ice
Student pro-democracy protesters in Tiananmen Square, China construct a replica of the Statue of Liberty, naming it the Goddess of Democracy

Bing CrosbyrecordsIrving Berlin's song "White Christmas" with the John Scott Trotter Orchestra and the Ken Darby Singers in just 18 minutes; it becomes the world's best-selling single, with an estimated 100 million copies sold
Extreme heatwave in northern and central India with unconfirmed record temperature reading of 52.3 C (126.14 F), later downgraded to 49 C (120.2 F)
KingCharles Iof England attempts to escape captivity at Carisbrooke Castle, Isle of Wight, for second time by hacksawing through a barred window but caught in the act
Charles Stritefiles patent for the automatic pop-up toaster
Trademark "Escalator" registered by Otis Elevator Co.
Patrick Henry's historic speech against the Stamp Act, answering a cry of "Treason!" with, "If this be treason, make the most of it!"
Australian Sue Prell first female golfer to hit consecutive holes-in one; 13th and 14th holes at Chatswood Golf Club, Sydney
MLB Philadelphia Phillies Dick Allen hits 529' HR out of Connie Mack Stadium in Philadelphia
Death toll on Puerto Rico 70 times higher than official figure, likely 4,600 died from Hurricane Maria according to Harvard University study
Igor Stravinsky's avant-garde ballet score "Le Sacre du Printemps" (The Rite of Spring) for the Ballets Russes premieres at the Théâtre des Champs-Élysées in Paris, provoking a riot

SingerDavid Bowiejoins guitaristDavid Gilmouras special guest during encore at Royal Albert Hall, London concert; they perform "Arnold Layne" and "Comfortably Numb" in what becomes Bowie's final UK appearance
Doug Scott, English mountaineer (first ascent of south-west face of Mount Everest), born in Nottingham, England (d. 2020)[1]
Peter II becomes Tsar of Russia aged 11
Thomas Bradley elected 1st African American mayor of Los Angeles, California
World War II Memorial is dedicated in Washington, D.C.
U.S. Supreme Court rules that disabled golfer Casey Martin can use a cart to ride in tournaments
Hong Kong student activist group Scholarism started byJoshua Wongand Ivan Lam
Dorothea Dixoffers help in setting up hospitals for the Union Army
Ebenezer Butterick, American inventor (tissue paper dress pattern), born in Sterling, Massachusetts (d. 1903)
Sylvester Magee, last living American slave and oldest person who ever lived, born in North Carolina (d. 1971)
Peter Higgs, English theoretical physicist (Nobel Prize for Physics, 2013), born in Newcastle upon Tyne, England (d. 2024)[1]
Ralph Metcalfe, American athlete, politician (Rep-D-Ill; joint world record 100m, Olympic gold 4x100m relay 1936), born in Atlanta, Georgia (d. 1978)
William Yorzyk Jr., American swimmer (Olympic gold 200m butterfly, WR 2:19.3, 1956), born in Northampton, Massachusetts (d. 2020)
Jai Hindley becomes only the second Australian cyclist to win a Grand Tour with a 1' 18" victory over 2019 champion Richard Carapaz in the Giro d'Italia
Super Rugby Final, Orlando Stadium, Soweto: Bulls (Pretoria) successfully defend their title with a 25-17 win over the Stormers (Cape Town) in an all-South African final

In an all South African Super 14 Rugby final, the Bulls (Pretoria) retain title, 25-17 over the Stormers (Cape Town) at the Orlando Stadium, Soweto; Morné Steyn kicks 6 penalties and a conversion for the Bulls
Robin Ventura set a college baseball record with hits in 57 games
Graeme Hick scores his 1,000th run of 1st-class cricket season
Fifteen young women are fired by Curtis Publishing in Philadelphia for dancing the "Turkey Trot" during their lunch break
Norwegian ship Storstad collides with Canadian ship Empress of Ireland on St Lawrence River; 1,024 die
Paddington Station, London's terminus for the Great Western Railway opens, with a design byIsambard Kingdom Brunel
ElvisandPriscilla Presleyhold a wedding reception at Graceland for friends and family not at the original ceremony
Everly Brothers single "Cathy's Clown" hits #1
TheCarter Familydebut on the Grand Ole Opry with Chet Atkins
Car tune "One Piece At A Time" byJohnny Cashhits #29
"Court Room" by Clarence Carter hits #61
British pop singerFreddie Mercuryand Spanish opera diva Montserrat Caballé debut their song "Barcelona" at the Ibiza Fest
Atlantic Records releases the eponymous debut album of "Crosby, Stills & Nash"; it contains hits  'Marrakesh Express' and 'Suite: Judy Blue Eyes'
